title: Dysuria/UTI symptoms

presenting_history.text: Defines DHA-licensed telehealth dysuria scope and qualified applicability.
presenting_history.rationale: Supports mapped dysuria presentation identity within qualified DHA telehealth scope.
presenting_history.evidence_extract: Defines DHA-licensed telehealth dysuria scope and qualified applicability.

source.title: Clinical Guidelines for Virtual Management of Dysuria

red_flags.text: Supports fever with flank pain or tenderness, vomiting, visible haematuria, pregnancy, diabetes, immunosuppression, and recurrence as prompts only, without automatic urgency or referral output.
red_flags.rationale: Supports mapped red-flag prompts and flank or CVA tenderness documentation without automatic urgency.
red_flags.evidence_extract: Supports fever with flank pain or tenderness, vomiting, visible haematuria, pregnancy, diabetes, immunosuppression, and recurrence as prompts only, without automatic urgency or referral output.

source.title: Clinical Guidelines for Virtual Management of Dysuria

investigations.text: Supports urinalysis, recommendation-qualified urine culture, and renal-function or electrolyte review in qualified pyelonephritis, nausea, or vomiting contexts.
investigations.rationale: Supports mapped urinalysis, qualified culture, and qualified renal-function review documentation.
investigations.evidence_extract: Supports urinalysis, recommendation-qualified urine culture, and renal-function or electrolyte review in qualified pyelonephritis, nausea, or vomiting contexts.

source.title: Clinical Guidelines for Virtual Management of Dysuria

medication.text: Supports onset, persistence, severity, duration, location, fever, rigors, nausea, vomiting, flank pain, medicine or procedure context, pregnancy and sexual context, trauma or irritants, haematuria, frequency, urgency, and voiding symptoms.
medication.rationale: Supports mapped urinary symptom, duration, fever, flank pain, vomiting, haematuria, pregnancy, and history-draft fields.
medication.evidence_extract: Supports onset, persistence, severity, duration, location, fever, rigors, nausea, vomiting, flank pain, medicine or procedure context, pregnancy and sexual context, trauma or irritants, haematuria, frequency, urgency, and voiding symptoms.

source_limitations: The workflow does not encode all age, sex, pregnancy, recurrence, comorbidity, telehealth, and complicated-infection qualifiers.

Urinary-retention prompts, recurrent UTI in a male, broad blood tests, CBC, CRP, imaging, most physical examination fields, hydration advice, medicine plan, recurrent-UTI plan, generic clinician plan, safety-net wording, follow-up, fixed intervals, and PRN wording remain unsupported.
Diagnosis, infection classification, antibiotic or other treatment, medicine dose, imaging, referral urgency, and follow-up timing remain clinician responsibilities.
All prior supported legacy-item mappings were removed because canonical item-level applicability and workflow-specific rationale were not present in explicit repository records.
curation_policy: Direct guideline curation: retain only explicitly mapped content; rewrite only with explicit item mapping; remove unmapped legacy content; add only committed exact-section evidence summaries.
